author: codex-root

patch.value: Roadmap horizon and promotion policy: outcome-based portfolio horizons with evidence-gated movement

Define one durable portfolio policy for organizing immediate foundations, scale expansion, universal-platform capabilities, and exploratory research without speculative calendar promises or duplicate initiative trees.

The policy defines Current, Emerging, Expansion, and Exploratory horizons by evidence and dependency readiness rather than dates; Every horizon entry references an existing canonical item or passes an all-status duplicate check before creation; Promotion requires explicit acceptance evidence, predecessor state, scale or quality proof where applicable, and an append-only decision comment; Demotion, splitting, merging, rejection, and supersedence preserve prior rationale in immutable history; Cross-cutting quality, security, documentation, SDK parity, graph completeness, and token-efficiency gates apply to every horizon; The living milestone can render a bounded horizon summary without replacing detailed item metadata or creating parallel backlogs
Keep the multi-decade roadmap understandable, adaptable, and traceable while every independently deliverable capability remains a canonical item
Agents and maintainers can see what matures next, why it is sequenced there, and what evidence promotes it without reading the full historical corpus
Reduces duplicate roadmap items, premature implementation, hidden prerequisites, and calendar-driven commitments while preserving ambitious future planning
A living outcome-based horizon map links canonical initiatives, promotion evidence, dependencies, risks, and review triggers from current foundations through long-term universal-platform research

The ecosystem now spans command simplification, SDK-first architecture, million-item context, graph analytics, immutable history, multi-agent safety, CI, VCS, digital twins, federation, signatures, and policy; the existing initiatives need one explicit promotion model.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check: all-status searches for roadmap horizon, portfolio sequencing, now-next-later, exploratory planning, and promotion policy found the living milestone pm-doxj and initiative-specific notes but no decision governing evidence-based movement across the portfolio. This item defines governance only; it does not duplicate any delivery feature, epic, or prior point-in-time review.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Initial horizon model: Current = command grammar, SDK boundary completion, active graph/context/governance foundations; Emerging = derived index, evaluation, memory tiers, million-item and concurrency proof; Expansion = universal SDK exemplars, cross-workspace federation, signatures, role policy, event coordination; Exploratory = temporal digital twins and accepted cognitive-context subsystems. Quality, security, documentation, immutable proof, and token budgets are continuous constraints rather than a separate terminal phase.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Long-range planning stays useful when horizons are promotion states backed by evidence, not promised dates. Canonical work items remain the source of execution truth; the portfolio decision supplies only sequencing and promotion semantics.

message: Create evidence-gated roadmap horizon policy after all-status duplicate review

author: codex-root
message: Link tracker integrity verification to roadmap governance

author: codex-root

author: codex-root
message: Link parent-scope duplicate verification to roadmap governance

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Baseline evidence: root help currently exposes 75 top-level names while contracts summary exposes 63 compact command summaries. The established pm-n7rr partition already assigns every spelling to a canonical noun family, hidden alias, internal helper, extension-owned surface, or generated discovery path. Promotion policy therefore treats simplification as delivery against existing canonical items, never as feature removal or a fresh command taxonomy.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-22: post-organization tracker totals are 1,914 items, 1,922 history streams, and 32,188 events before this evidence comment. Validate is ok with zero history drift; the known terminal-resolution warning remains 277 items under pm-e9zh and the five historical unknown-author events remain under pm-h90s. Graph audit reports 1,917 nodes and 3,366 edges, 223 active nodes, zero active isolates, zero active degree-one nodes, and only the same informational terminal legacy findings. Exact/fuzzy duplicate results remain the known canceled probe cluster and parent-scope duplicate count is zero. All active non-root items have acceptance criteria, estimates, tags, and parents. pnpm build, changelog verification, contracts verification, and the full static quality gate passed, including package boundary and token-budget gates.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Publication evidence: substantive tracker organization commit 068b1e3d013c15eb23d952c484be075700fc65a6 was pushed directly to origin/main as explicitly requested, without a PR or review workflow. GitHub reported the expected required-status-check bypass for the direct push. Exact local HEAD and origin/main matched after fetch. gh run list for that commit returned no workflow runs, consistent with tracker-only path filtering; local build, changelog, contracts, static-quality, package-boundary, token-budget, tracker validation, graph, and duplicate checks are recorded above.
